title: Top health habits to thrive in 2023: Paul Saladino on Radical Health Radio

chunks.title: Introduction to Radical Health Radio
chunks.summary: The host introduces the new podcast, Radical Health Radio, and shares excitement about discussing the animal-based diet with Paul Saladino, emphasizing a more approachable format for health enthusiasts.
chunks.transcript: and the reason I draw the parallel is because I think that we are domesticated humans I've talked about this we live in a human zoo and there are ways to step outside the cage doors the cage doors are open you could just there are ways to do this but it it means bringing the ideas of how we've lived in the past into our quote modern live this week's podcast is a special one I'm super excited to share my appearance on heart and soil's new podcast which is called radical Health radio what else would it be called right we found an amazing host is St you'll hear us talking on the podcast this week and we get into some really good questions they had some great questions for me and I really enjoyed hanging out with the crew from hard and soil they're doing an amazing job with this podcast so there's a ton of podcasts in the world but I think you should check out radical Health radio we've got a YouTube channel for radical Health radio on YouTube or you can find Radical Health radio wherever you listen to podcast but enjoy this appearance of mine on heart and soil's new podcast radical Health radio like I said we answer all kinds of burning questions we talk about overarching issues how I learn how I think about medicine and health this is a solid one guys enjoy and check out our new podcast radical Health radio wherever you listen to podcast you're G to find a lot of value there as well and I think that as I would say radical Health radio will probably be a more digestible less technical version of a podcast hard and soil has come up with a lot of great topics and a lot of great guests and I know my podcast can get kind of technical sometimes radical Health radio is going to be very approachable for all of you so enjoy see you

chunks.title: What Does Radical Health Mean?
chunks.summary: Paul Saladino discusses his interpretation of radical health, likening it to a vibrant life filled with energy, joy, and fulfilling relationships. He emphasizes the difference between a disease management system and a holistic approach to health.

chunks.title: Paul Saladino's Journey
chunks.summary: Paul shares his personal journey from being a ski bum to becoming a physician assistant, and then a medical doctor, revealing how past experiences and health challenges led him to explore nutrition deeply and advocate for an animal-based diet.
chunks.transcript: unexpected but uh I'm really grateful to get to do it because I get to do something that is really fun it's fun to connect the dots and do creative work and feel like it's quite was never expected I I you know when I got out College I was a ski bum so my my original Vision was I'm GNA travel around the world and the United States and Ski and mountain bike and I'm just gonna live and I threw hike to the Pacific Crest Trail and spent a lot of time in the wilderness and and then eventually I kind of had the itch that that grew somewhere and I I kind of like biochemistry I kind of like medicine I think I'll go back to PA school so became a physician assistant but immediately didn't like it because I wasn't able to think for myself I was mostly just doing things that other doctors told me to do and that's a that's an important role that physician assistants and nurse practitioners have and I think that as I grow As I Grew in my physician assistant career I had more autonomy but still I wasn't able to really profoundly affect the Paradigm in in the way that I suddenly realized I wanted to so then I went back to medical school and then I went to residency and during medical school and residency I had some sense that I was not quite like my peers and I don't know if that was because I was older or I had more experiences or I had fallen down mountains and flooded rivers and been caught in Avalanches or what it was in my past that shaped my slight iconoclasm or my tendency toward being iconoclastic just I don't know I I like to see things differently and I like to connect the dots and I like to really drill down and and ask what was at the root of things and and the paradigms that I was presented with in medical school and residency were just not that interesting so I I kind of knew that I was probably not going to do things a traditional way which got me in trouble a few times but um I think organically it just ended up being a part of my journey that I was becoming more and more interested in nutrition and more and more interested in the way I was eating and how that affected my personal health many people will know that I had eczema and Asthma a IC conditions throughout medical school and residency and even before that and so I was just increasingly fascinated by why the heck these were happening and what was causing it and I started iterating on my food and that was the beginning of really my journey many many years ago the first iteration was vegan and then paleo and then paleo for probably almost a decade and then carnivore and then now animal-based so it's it's been an interesting thing and it's I think I'll just emphasize again completely unexpected but super exciting that people are finding value in it which is the best thing that I can say um is happening with with the amount of uh with the amount of reach that it that it's gained yeah it's really incredible

chunks.title: Living Outside the Box: A Non-Dogmatic Approach
chunks.summary: Paul emphasizes the importance of questioning traditional dietary beliefs and being open to evolving one’s perspective based on new evidence, contrasting conventional medical training with a more holistic and adaptable approach to diet and health.
